GATTEY FILED BY DEFENDANT MONDAL, SANTANU , AN INDIVIDUAL KATDARE, ASMITA , AN INDIVIDUAL SAINGER, SHIVENDRA SINGH , AN INDIVIDUAL POSITIVEEDGE SOLUTIONS, LLC

LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS SANTANU MONDAL, ASMITA KATDARE AND SHIVENDRA SAINGER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED; 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CERTAIN CAUSES OF ACTION. CA. HAS DECLARED UNLAWFUL THE NONCOMPETITION CLAUSE CENTRAL TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. APPLICATION GROUP INC V. HUNTER GROUP, INC (1998) 61 CAL APP 4TH 881, 902; KOLANI V. GLUSKA (1998) 64 CAL APP 4TH 402. LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION TO PLEAD A CAUSE OF ACTION UNDER THE UNIFORM TRADE SECRETS ACT, CA. CIVIL CODE SECTION 3426.1 ET SEQ. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM BASED ON MARYLAND LAW FAILS. LEAVE TO AMEND IS DENIED AS TO THE 2ND, 3RD, 4TH AND 13TH CAUSES OF ACTION. BUSINESS & PROFESSIONS CODE SECTION 16600 RENDERS THE CLAUSE AT ISSUE UNLAWFUL. LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED AS TO THE 5TH, 8TH, 10TH AND THE 11TH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD CONDUCT UNRELATED TO MISAPPROPRIATION OF TRADE SECRETS. THE DECISIONS IN CALLAWAY GOLF V. DUNLAP SLAZENGER GROUP AMERICAS, INC (D DEL 2004) 318 F SUPP 216 AND DIGITAL ENVOY, INC V. GOOGLE INC (ND CAL 2005) 370 F SUPP 1025 MAKE CLEAR THAT THE FACTS ALLEGED ARE PREEMPTED BY THE UNIFORM TRADE SECRETS ACT CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COMPLAINT APPEARS TO REFLECT THAT PLAINTIFF MAY BE ABLE TO ALLEGE OTHER FACTS APART FROM THE MISAPPROPRIATION CLAIM. LEAVE TO AMEND IS DENIED AS TO THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE DECISION IN FIFIELD V. FINSTON (1960) 54 CAL. 2D 632 BARS THE CLAIM. THE COURT GRANTED PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE BREACH OF CONTRACT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT POSITIVEEDGE SOLUTIONS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED; 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CERTAIN CAUSES OF ACTION. LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION TO PLEAD A CAUSE OF ACTION UNDER THE UNIFORM TRADE SECRETS ACT. LEAVE TO AMEND IS DENIED AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRTEENTH CAUSES OF ACTION. POSITIVEEDGE IS NOT A PARTY TO THE CONTRACT AND THE PROVISION AT ISSUE IS UNLAWFUL UNDER BUSINESS & PROFESSIONS CODE SECTION 16600. LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED AS TO THE FIFTH, EIGHTH, TENTH AND ELEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION TO PLEAD CONDUCT UNRELATED TO THE MISAPPROPRIATION CLAIM. PLAINTIFF ALSO MUST PLEAD MORE SPECIFIC FACTS TO CLARIFY THE SERVICESOFT CONTRACT. LEAVE TO AMEND IS DENIED AS TO THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT GRANTED PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE BREACH OF CONTRACT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF JAYPAR, INC'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE PROBABLE IRREPARABLE HARM. IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF WAS ON NOTICE OF A POSSIBLE CLAIM ON JUNE 8, AND WAITED UNTIL AUGUST 22, TO FILE THE COMPLAINT AND WAITED NEARLY TWO MORE MONTHS TO SEEK INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. PROBABILITY OF SUCCESS IS UNCERTAIN. THE COVENANTS NOT TO COMPETE ARE NOT ENFORCEABLE UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW AND PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO IDENTIFY WITH ANY SPECIFICITY A TRADE SECRET ALLEGEDLY TAKEN; RATHER, PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT AND DECLARATION ARE REPLETE WITH GENERALITIES AND THE MERE IDENTIFY OF A CUSTOMER ALREADY DISCLOSED ON PLAINTIFF'S WEB SITE. THE EQUITIES FAVOR DEFENDANTS FOR ANY INJUNCTION WOULD LIKELY PUT DEFENDANTS OUT OF BUSINESS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Scott Gattey of Gattey Law Offices (650.596.7123) and Paul Byrne of Cornerstone Law Group (357.2094) for defendant and moving party
Al Mohajerian and Michael Smith of Mohajerian Law Corp (310.556.3800) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: DEFENDANT POSITIVEEDGE SOLUTIONS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained; 10 days leave to amend as to certain causes of action. Leave to amend is granted as to the first cause of action to plead a cause of action under the Uniform Trade Secrets Act. Leave to amend is denied as to the second and thirteenth causes of action. Positiveedge is not a party to the contract and the provision at issue is unlawful under Business & Professions Code section 16600. Leave to amend is granted as to the fifth, eighth, tenth and eleventh causes of action to plead conduct unrelated to the misappropriation claim. Plaintiff also must plead more specific facts to clarify the Servicesoft contract. Leave to amend is denied as to the ninth cause of action. The Court granted plaintiff's request for leave to amend to allege breach of contract. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/pb) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Plaintiff Jaypar, Inc'S Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Oct-30-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Scott Gattey of Gattey Law Offices (650.596.7123) and Paul Byrne of Cornerstone Law Group (357.2094) for defendants and responding parties
Al Mohajerian and Michael Smith of Mohajerian Law Corp (310.556.3800) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF JAYPAR, INC'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is denied. Plaintiff has failed to demonstrate probable irreparable harm. It appears that plaintiff was on notice of a possible claim on June 8, and waited until August 22, to file the complaint and waited nearly two more months to seek injunctive relief. Probability of success is uncertain. The covenants not to compete are not enforceable under California law and plaintiff has failed to identify with any specificity a trade secret allegedly taken; rather, plaintiff's complaint and declaration are replete with generalities and the mere identify of a customer already disclosed on plaintiff's web site. The equities favor defendants for any injunction would likely put defendants out of business. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Scott Gattey of Gattey Law Offices (650.596.7123) and Paul Byrne of Cornerstone Law Group (357.2094) for defendants and moving parties
Al Mohajerian and Michael Smith of Mohajerian Law Corp (310.556.3800) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and responding party
Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS SANTANU MONDAL, ASMITA KATDARE AND SHIVENDRA SAINGER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained; 10 days leave to amend as to certain causes of action. Ca. has declared unlawful the noncompetition clause central to plaintiff's claim. Application Group Inc v. Hunter Group, Inc (1998) 61 Cal App 4th 881, 902; Kolani v. Gluska (1998) 64 Cal App 4th 402. Leave to amend is granted as to the first cause of action to plead a cause of action under the Uniform Trade Secrets Act, Ca. Civil Code section 3426.1 et seq. Plaintiff's claim based on Maryland law fails. Leave to amend is denied as to the 2nd, 3rd, 4th and 13th causes of action. Business & Professions Code section 16600 renders the clause at issue unlawful. Leave to amend is granted as to the 5th, 8th, 10th and the 11th causes of action for plaintiff to plead conduct unrelated to misappropriation of trade secrets. The decisions in Callaway Golf v. Dunlap Slazenger Group Americas, Inc (D Del 2004) 318 F Supp 216 and Digital Envoy, Inc v. Google Inc (ND Cal 2005) 370 F Supp 1025 make clear that the facts alleged are preempted by the Uniform Trade Secrets Act cause of action. The complaint appears to reflect that plaintiff may be able to allege other facts apart from the misappropriation claim. Leave to amend is denied as to the ninth cause of action. The decision in Fifield v. Finston (1960) 54 Cal. 2d 632 bars the claim. The Court granted plaintiff's request for leave to amend to allege breach of contract. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion In Lieu Of An Answer (C.C.P. 1281.7) To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceeding; Appendix Of Non-California Authorities In Support Of Motion In Lieu Of Answer
Off Calendar Oct-21-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Von Loewenfeldt of Kerr & Wagstaffe (371.8500) for defendant and moving party
Michael T. Smith of Mohajerian Law Corp. (310.556.3800) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and responding party

Discussed and the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT SERVICESOURCE INTERNATIONAL, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDING is granted. This stay is as to defendant Service Source International, LLC only. The case shall proceed as to all other defendants. The Court is not making a finding as to which body of law applies; that remains up to the arbitrator. Order signed in open Court. (302/HEK/JW) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
